event: history of primary refractory acute myeloblastic leukemia
allogeneic bone marrow transplant from cord blood
CT scan revealed segmental ischemia of the small bowel
anemic with a hemoglobin of 7 g/L
thrombocytopenic with a platelet count of 10 x 10^9 /L
decision made to proceed to the operating room for an exploratory laparotomy
approximately 50 cm of small bowel resected
primary anastomosis performed with a stapler device
necrotic bowel located in the mid-jejunum
admitted to the intensive care unit
amphotericin B added to his antimicrobials
repeat CT scan showed an area of necrotic small bowel remote from the initial anastomosis
goals of care discussions with family and oncology team
